---
title: "Simulator"
subtitle: "Live in-browser Monte Carlo on parameters of your choosing"
---

Every slider on this page kicks off a fresh Monte Carlo in your
browser; the [Model](model.qmd) page defines the symbols. Two
things are slightly different here. First, the desk is split into
zero-capital **operating books** (fee, b2b, retained, switching —
each with $P\!\&\!L(0) = 0$, every tonne sourced at spot) and an
**active treasury** parameterised by raw inventory state $(k,
C_{\mathrm{basis}})$ — kVCM tokens on hand plus their sunk USD cost.
Any strategy's desk P&L is `operating + treasury` evaluated
sample-wise; the custom-desk histogram below shows $b2b + \text{treasury}$
at your slider settings. Second, the price process is always the
compensated Merton jump-diffusion; set the three jump sliders
$(\lambda_J, \mu_J, \sigma_J)$ to zero for pure GBM.

::: {.side-panel-left .side-panel .pc-controls}
### Price process

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof pi0 = Inputs.range([0, 500], { step: 1, value: 125, label: "π₀ (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof P = Inputs.range([100, 25000], { step: 10, value: 2160, label: "P (kVCM / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof mu = Inputs.range([-1, 1], { step: 0.005, value: -0.1, label: "drift μ (annualised)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sigma2 = Inputs.range([0, 3], { step: 0.005, value: 0.06, label: "variance σ² (annualised)" })
```

### Jumps (Merton)

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaJ = Inputs.range([0, 50], { step: 0.1, value: 20, label: "jump intensity λ_J (/yr)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof muJ = Inputs.range([-0.5, 0.5], { step: 0.01, value: -0.05, label: "mean log-jump μ_J" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sigmaJ = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.35, label: "log-jump SD σ_J" })
```

### Demand & inventory

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaDay = Inputs.range([0, 50], { step: 0.1, value: 1, label: "retirements (tonnes / day)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof kPre = Inputs.range([0, 2000000], { step: 1000, value: 300000, label: "initial inventory (kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof cBasis = Inputs.range([0, 1000000], { step: 100, value: 21000, label: "inventory basis (USD, total)" })
```

### Contract

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Tdays = Inputs.range([30, 1095], { step: 5, value: 365, label: "horizon T (days)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Q = Inputs.range([0, 1000], { step: 0.5, value: 180, label: "fixed quote Q (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof fee = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "fee rate f" })
```

### Syndication

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof beta = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0, label: "cession β" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof theta = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0, label: "counterparty load θ" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof premiumMode = Inputs.radio(["sharpe", "cvar"], { value: "sharpe", label: "premium mode" })
```

### Switching

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof barrierRatio = Inputs.range([1, 3], { step: 0.01, value: 3, label: "switching barrier h = H/S₀ (3 ≈ no switch on most runs)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof feePostLocked = Inputs.toggle({ label: "lock f_post to f", value: true })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof feePostSlider = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "post-switch fee rate f_post" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// null when locked (simulateSwitching interprets this as "use inputs.fee");
// a numeric value when the user wants an independent post-switch rate.
feePost = feePostLocked ? null : feePostSlider
```

### Monte Carlo

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof nPaths = Inputs.select([2000, 5000, 10000, 20000, 40000], { value: 5000, label: "paths" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof nSteps = Inputs.select([50, 100, 200, 365], { value: 100, label: "steps / path" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof seed = Inputs.range([1, 9999], { step: 1, value: 42, label: "PRNG seed" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
mcResetButton = {
  const defaults = {
    pi0: 125, P: 2160, mu: -0.1, sigma2: 0.06,
    lambdaJ: 20, muJ: -0.05, sigmaJ: 0.35,
    lambdaDay: 1, kPre: 300000, cBasis: 21000,
    Tdays: 365, Q: 180, fee: 0.4,
    beta: 0, theta: 0, premiumMode: "sharpe",
    barrierRatio: 3, feePostLocked: true, feePostSlider: 0.4,
    nPaths: 5000, nSteps: 100, seed: 42,
  };
  const views = {
    pi0: viewof pi0, P: viewof P, mu: viewof mu, sigma2: viewof sigma2,
    lambdaJ: viewof lambdaJ, muJ: viewof muJ, sigmaJ: viewof sigmaJ,
    lambdaDay: viewof lambdaDay, kPre: viewof kPre, cBasis: viewof cBasis,
    Tdays: viewof Tdays, Q: viewof Q, fee: viewof fee,
    beta: viewof beta, theta: viewof theta, premiumMode: viewof premiumMode,
    barrierRatio: viewof barrierRatio, feePostLocked: viewof feePostLocked,
    feePostSlider: viewof feePostSlider,
    nPaths: viewof nPaths, nSteps: viewof nSteps, seed: viewof seed,
  };
  const wrap = document.createElement("div");
  wrap.className = "pc-reset";
  const btn = document.createElement("button");
  btn.type = "button";
  btn.textContent = "Reset to defaults";
  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    for (const k of Object.keys(defaults)) {
      const el = views[k];
      el.value = defaults[k];
      el.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
    }
  });
  wrap.appendChild(btn);
  return wrap;
}
```

:::

## Derived quantities

Closed-form summaries of the slider state: $S_0 = \pi_0 / P$, horizon
$T$ in years, $N = \lambda T$ total tonnes, coverage $\alpha =
\min(1, k / (N P))$, and the break-even quote $Q^* = (1 + f) P S_0
\cdot (e^{\mu T} - 1) / (\mu T)$ at which the fee and back-to-back
books share expected P&L.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
derived = {
  const sigma = Math.sqrt(sigma2);
  const S0 = P > 0 ? pi0 / P : 0;
  const T = Tdays / 365;
  const lambda = lambdaDay * 365;
  const N = lambda * T;
  const tokensNeeded = N * P;
  const coverageDays = (lambda > 0 && P > 0) ? kPre / (lambda * P) * 365 : Infinity;
  const coverageFrac = tokensNeeded > 0 ? Math.min(1, kPre / tokensNeeded) : 0;
  const impliedCost = kPre > 0 ? cBasis / kPre : 0;
  const QStarRatio = expm1OverX(mu * T);
  const QStar = (1 + fee) * P * S0 * QStarRatio;
  return { sigma, S0, T, lambda, N, tokensNeeded, coverageDays, coverageFrac, impliedCost, QStar };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="derived-stats">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">σ (volatility)</div><div class="value">${derived.sigma.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">S₀ = π₀ / P (USD / kVCM)</div><div class="value">${"$" + derived.S0.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">T (years)</div><div class="value">${derived.T.toFixed(3)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">N = λ·T (tonnes over horizon)</div><div class="value">${derived.N.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">tokens needed N·P</div><div class="value">${derived.tokensNeeded.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">coverage from inventory</div><div class="value">${(derived.coverageFrac * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">inventory runs out at (days)</div><div class="value">${isFinite(derived.coverageDays) ? Math.min(derived.coverageDays, Tdays).toFixed(1) : "∞"}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">implied basis per token</div><div class="value">${kPre > 0 ? "$" + derived.impliedCost.toFixed(3) : "—"}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">break-even Q* (USD / tonne)</div><div class="value">${"$" + derived.QStar.toFixed(3)}</div></div>
</div>`
```

`implied basis per token` is $C_{\mathrm{basis}} / k$ versus $S_0$:
above $S_0$, existing inventory sits on an unrealised loss; below,
an unrealised gain. It translates the custom book's P&L distribution
without touching its shape.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
import {
  expm1OverX, xTicksForHorizon, simulate, summarise, simulateSwitching,
  formatTickCurrency,
} from "./lib/compiled/src/core/index.js"
```

## Monte Carlo run

A run card (paths, wall time, coverage, leftover tokens), a summary
table, histograms, and sample price paths follow. Wall time stays
sub-second at 5,000–10,000 paths; at 40,000 the bottleneck is
re-plotting, not the simulation itself. The [Merton compensation
identity](model.qmd#compensated-merton-jump-diffusion) tells you
what to expect when sweeping the jump sliders: the black mean rule
on each histogram holds position (means survive) while SD, VaR, and
CVaR widen as the jumps land.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
run = {
  const inputs = {
    S0: derived.S0, mu, sigma: derived.sigma, P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee, kPre, cBasis,
    beta, premiumLoad: theta, premiumMode,
    lambdaJ, muJ, sigmaJ,
    nPaths, nSteps, seed,
    keepPaths: 20,
  };
  const t0 = performance.now();
  const raw = simulate(inputs);
  const elapsedMs = performance.now() - t0;
  // Custom-desk samples = b2b + treasury at the slider's (k, C_basis).
  const customDeskSamples = new Float64Array(raw.b2bSamples.length);
  for (let i = 0; i < customDeskSamples.length; i++) {
    customDeskSamples[i] = raw.b2bSamples[i] + raw.treasurySamples[i];
  }
  return {
    inputs,
    ...raw,
    customDeskSamples,
    fee: summarise(raw.feeSamples),
    b2b: summarise(raw.b2bSamples),
    retained: summarise(raw.retainedSamples),
    treasury: summarise(raw.treasurySamples),
    customDesk: summarise(customDeskSamples),
    elapsedMs,
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Sibling run of the switching operating book. The switching book is itself
// zero-capital (starts at P&L(0) = 0 and sources every tonne at spot); the
// matched-desk variant is `switching + treasury(N·P, N·P·S₀)` sample-wise,
// which we don't surface on this page — see Validation for composed desks.
switchRun = {
  const inputs = {
    S0: derived.S0, mu, sigma: derived.sigma, P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee,
    barrierRatio, feePost,
    lambdaJ, muJ, sigmaJ,
    nPaths, nSteps, seed,
    keepPaths: 0,
  };
  const t0 = performance.now();
  const raw = simulateSwitching(inputs);
  const elapsedMs = performance.now() - t0;
  let switched = 0, tauSum = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < raw.switchedMask.length; i++) {
    switched += raw.switchedMask[i];
    tauSum += raw.tauSamples[i];
  }
  return {
    inputs, ...raw, elapsedMs,
    summary: summarise(raw.pnlSamples),
    probSwitch: switched / raw.switchedMask.length,
    expectedTau: tauSum / raw.tauSamples.length,
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="derived-stats">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">paths simulated</div><div class="value">${nPaths.toLocaleString()}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">wall time</div><div class="value">${run.elapsedMs.toFixed(1)} ms</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">coverage this run</div><div class="value">${(run.tauFrac * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">leftover tokens at T</div><div class="value">${run.tokensLeftover.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">cession β</div><div class="value">${(beta * 100).toFixed(0)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">premium π_syn received</div><div class="value">${(run.premium >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(run.premium).toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">custom desk CVaR₉₅</div><div class="value">${(run.customDesk.cvar95 >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(run.customDesk.cvar95).toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">custom desk SD vs. b2b</div><div class="value">${run.b2b.sd > 0 ? (100 * (1 - run.customDesk.sd / run.b2b.sd)).toFixed(1) + "%" : "—"}</div></div>
</div>`
```

When the barrier slider sits below 3 the switching book is active; the
diagnostic cards below report $\mathbb{P}[\tau < T]$ (empirical switch
probability over the MC paths) and $\mathbb{E}[\tau \wedge T]$
(expected barrier-crossing time, with $a \wedge b := \min(a, b)$ so
paths that never cross contribute $T$).

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`${(isFinite(barrierRatio) && barrierRatio < 3)
  ? `<div class="derived-stats">
    <div class="card"><div class="label">switching barrier h</div><div class="value">${barrierRatio.toFixed(2)} (H = $${(barrierRatio * derived.S0).toFixed(4)})</div></div>
    <div class="card"><div class="label">f_post</div><div class="value">${(feePost ?? fee).toFixed(3)}${feePost === null ? " (locked to f)" : ""}</div></div>
    <div class="card"><div class="label">P[τ &lt; T]</div><div class="value">${(switchRun.probSwitch * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div></div>
    <div class="card"><div class="label">E[τ]</div><div class="value">${(switchRun.expectedTau * 365 / derived.T).toFixed(1)} days</div></div>
    <div class="card"><div class="label">switching E[Π]</div><div class="value">${(switchRun.summary.mean >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(switchRun.summary.mean).toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
    <div class="card"><div class="label">switching CVaR₉₅</div><div class="value">${(switchRun.summary.cvar95 >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(switchRun.summary.cvar95).toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  </div>`
  : `<div class="derived-stats"><div class="card"><div class="label">switching</div><div class="value">disabled (h ≈ ∞) — set the slider below 3 to activate</div></div></div>`
}`
```

### Summary statistics

One row per **operating book** (fee, b2b, retained, switching) plus
one row for the **treasury** at the slider's $(k, C_{\mathrm{basis}})$
and a derived **custom desk** row $= b2b + \text{treasury}$. The
switching row appears only when the barrier slider sits below 3.

| column | meaning |
| --- | --- |
| $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$ (mc) | sample mean |
| SD | sample standard deviation |
| $\pm \mathrm{CI}_{95}$ | 95% half-width of the mean, $1.96 \cdot \mathrm{SD} / \sqrt{n}$ |
| $\mathrm{VaR}_{95} / \mathrm{VaR}_{99}$ | 5% / 1% loss thresholds |
| $\mathrm{CVaR}_{95} / \mathrm{CVaR}_{99}$ | tail-mean losses inside those thresholds |
| $\mathbb{P}[\Pi < 0]$ | probability of a losing realisation |
| Sharpe | $\mathbb{E}[\Pi] / \mathrm{SD}[\Pi]$ (horizon-absolute, not annualised) |

P&L is the USD total over $[0, T]$; divide by $N = \lambda T$ for a
per-tonne figure. Three things to watch as you sweep:

- **The treasury row** opens at MTM $k \cdot S_0 - C_{\mathrm{basis}}$
  and drifts as the inventory burns down — sweeping $C_{\mathrm{basis}}$
  translates the location without touching the shape.
- **The custom-desk row** collapses to the matched limit at
  $k = N P$, $C_{\mathrm{basis}} = N P S_0$: $I_T$ cancels
  between `b2b` and `treasury` consumption and the desk total
  becomes deterministic $N (Q - P S_0)$.
- **$k = 0$** zeroes the treasury sample-wise, so the custom desk
  collapses onto `b2b`.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
summaryRows = [
  { book: "fee", ...run.fee },
  { book: "b2b", ...run.b2b },
  { book: "retained (syndicated-on-b2b)", ...run.retained },
  { book: "treasury (k, C_basis)", ...run.treasury },
  { book: "custom desk = b2b + treasury", ...run.customDesk },
  // Switching row only when the barrier is active.
  ...(isFinite(barrierRatio) && barrierRatio < 3
    ? [{ book: `switching (h=${barrierRatio.toFixed(2)})`, ...switchRun.summary }]
    : []),
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(summaryRows, {
  columns: ["book","mean","sd","ci95","var95","var99","cvar95","cvar99","probLoss","sharpe"],
  header: {
    book: "book",
    mean: "E[Π] (mc)",
    sd: "SD",
    ci95: "±CI95",
    var95: "VaR95",
    var99: "VaR99",
    cvar95: "CVaR95",
    cvar99: "CVaR99",
    probLoss: "P[Π<0]",
    sharpe: "Sharpe",
  },
  format: {
    mean: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    sd: x => "$" + x.toFixed(2),
    ci95: x => "±$" + x.toFixed(2),
    var95: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    var99: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    cvar95: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    cvar99: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    probLoss: x => x.toFixed(3),
    sharpe: x => x === null ? "—" : (x >= 0 ? "" : "−") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(3),
  }
})
```

### P&L distributions

One panel per book, terminal P&L on the x-axis. Solid black rule is
the MC mean; dashed grey rule is $\Pi = 0$. The fee panel is
non-negative and right-skewed (it inherits $I_T$'s right skew); the
b2b panel is the mirror. The treasury panel shifts linearly with
$C_{\mathrm{basis}}$ and widens with $k$. The custom-desk panel
(`b2b + treasury`) collapses to a spike at $k = N P$,
$C_{\mathrm{basis}} = N P S_0$ — the matched limit, with $I_T$
cancelling between the two summands.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Switching facet follows the same predicate as the summary table row
// (see the `summaryRows` cell above) so table and histogram agree in lockstep.
includeSwitch = isFinite(barrierRatio) && barrierRatio < 3
switchLabel = includeSwitch ? `switching (h=${barrierRatio.toFixed(2)})` : null
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pnlRecords = {
  const rows = [];
  // Subsample for plotting responsiveness.
  const stride = Math.max(1, Math.floor(run.feeSamples.length / 2500));
  for (let i = 0; i < run.feeSamples.length; i += stride) {
    rows.push({ book: "fee", pnl: run.feeSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "b2b", pnl: run.b2bSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "retained (syndicated-on-b2b)", pnl: run.retainedSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "treasury (k, C_basis)", pnl: run.treasurySamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "custom desk = b2b + treasury", pnl: run.customDeskSamples[i] });
    if (includeSwitch) rows.push({ book: switchLabel, pnl: switchRun.pnlSamples[i] });
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 900,
  height: 720,
  marginLeft: 80,
  marginRight: 30,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "terminal P&L (USD)", tickFormat: formatTickCurrency },
  y: { label: "empirical density" },
  fy: { label: "book" },
  color: { legend: true, domain: [
    "fee","b2b","retained (syndicated-on-b2b)","treasury (k, C_basis)","custom desk = b2b + treasury",
    ...(includeSwitch ? [switchLabel] : []),
  ] },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.ruleX([0], { stroke: "#888", strokeDasharray: "2 4" }),
    Plot.rectY(pnlRecords, Plot.binX({ y: "proportion" }, { x: "pnl", fill: "book", fy: "book", thresholds: 50 })),
    Plot.ruleX([
      { book: "fee", x: run.fee.mean },
      { book: "b2b", x: run.b2b.mean },
      { book: "retained (syndicated-on-b2b)", x: run.retained.mean },
      { book: "treasury (k, C_basis)", x: run.treasury.mean },
      { book: "custom desk = b2b + treasury", x: run.customDesk.mean },
      ...(includeSwitch ? [{ book: switchLabel, x: switchRun.summary.mean }] : []),
    ], { x: "x", fy: "book", stroke: "black", strokeWidth: 2 }),
  ]
})
```

_Solid black: MC mean. Dashed grey: $\Pi = 0$. The custom-desk
panel collapses to a spike as $k \to N P$ with
$C_{\mathrm{basis}} = N P S_0$ (matched limit, $I_T$ cancels between
b2b and treasury); the retained panel sits on top of the b2b panel
at $\beta = 0$ and collapses to a spike as $\beta \to 1$
(syndication limit)._

### Sample price paths and the inventory cliff

Twenty sampled kVCM trajectories (grey, first path highlighted
orange). The dashed green rule at $\alpha T$ marks inventory
exhaustion: the intermediary draws from inventory to the left of it
and buys at spot to the right. The custom book's randomness lives in
the right-of-rule region (plus a terminal $S_T$ contribution when
over-hedged). At $\alpha = 100\%$ the rule sits at or past $T$; at
$\alpha = 0$, at $t = 0$. Paths fan out like $\sigma\sqrt{t}$ — the
visual signature of GBM.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pathRecords = {
  const rows = [];
  const paths = run.sampledPaths;
  const nS = run.inputs.nSteps;
  for (let idx = 0; idx < paths.length; idx++) {
    const path = paths[idx];
    for (let k = 0; k <= nS; k++) {
      rows.push({
        tDays: (k / nS) * Tdays,
        S: path[k],
        id: idx,
      });
    }
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 900,
  height: 340,
  marginLeft: 70,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "t (days)", domain: [0, Tdays], ticks: xTicksForHorizon(Tdays) },
  y: { label: "kVCM spot S_t (USD)", zero: true, tickFormat: formatTickCurrency },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.line(pathRecords, { x: "tDays", y: "S", z: "id", stroke: "#888", strokeOpacity: 0.35 }),
    Plot.line(pathRecords.filter(r => r.id === 0), { x: "tDays", y: "S", stroke: "#c94", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    Plot.ruleX([run.tauFrac * Tdays], { stroke: "#2a7", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 2 }),
  ]
})
```

_Grey: sampled GBM trajectories of the kVCM spot. Orange: first path
highlighted. Green dashed rule: inventory-exhaustion time; right of
it, retirements are sourced at spot._
